item.title: UAE Secretly Carrying Out Attacks on Iran, WSJ Reports
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that the United Arab Emirates has been conducting covert attacks against Iran amid the ongoing conflict, marking a significant escalation of Gulf state involvement in the US-Iran confrontation.

item.title: Mossad Chief Warns Against Gofman Appointment in Letter to High Court
item.description: Outgoing Mossad chief sent a letter to Israel's High Court warning of "very great risk" from the appointment of Gofman, according to Haaretz. The warning comes amid significant security leadership transitions in Israel.

item.title: US Navy Submarine Arrives in Gibraltar as Iran Conflict Continues
item.description: A US Navy nuclear-powered submarine arrived in Gibraltar on Sunday, reinforcing Western naval presence in the Mediterranean amid ongoing tensions with Iran and operations in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Canada Eyes Turkey as Defense Industry Partner, Official Says
item.description: Canada is seeking lessons from Turkey's homegrown defense industrial ecosystem, with a senior Canadian official saying Ottawa looks to Ankara as a model for developing vital defense capabilities as a mid-power ally.

item.title: OPEC Oil Output at 26-Year Low Amid Iran War, Reuters Survey Shows
item.description: OPEC oil production has fallen to its lowest level in 26 years as the Iran conflict disrupts regional supply chains and the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to impact global energy markets.

item.title: Putin signals Ukraine war may be ending as Russia loses momentum
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested the war in Ukraine may be coming to an end, amid signs that Russia is losing battlefield momentum with Ukraine retaking territory and Russian casualties exceeding recruitment rates.

item.title: Nazi-looted painting found in home of Dutch SS leader descendants
item.description: A portrait stolen from Jewish art collector Jacques Goudstikker during World War II was discovered in the home of descendants of Hendrik Seyffardt, a Dutch SS general, after a family member came forward to return it.

item.title: Six migrants found dead in shipping container at Texas rail yard
item.description: Six people, believed to be migrants, were found dead inside a shipping container at a Union Pacific rail yard near the Mexico border in Laredo, Texas, with heat stroke suspected as the cause of death.

item.title: Last passengers evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship as new cases confirmed
item.description: The final passengers from the MV Hondius have been evacuated to 20 countries for quarantine, with new hantavirus cases confirmed in France and the US, as the WHO says the public health risk remains low.

item.title: Chad airstrikes on Boko Haram kill dozens of Nigerian fishermen
item.description: Fishermen's leader reports 40 people feared dead after Chad targeted Boko Haram militants near Lake Chad, with casualties including Nigerian civilians caught in strikes or drowning while fleeing.

item.title: Musk, Cook among CEOs to join Trump on China visit
item.description: Seventeen US executives including Elon Musk and Tim Cook are expected to accompany President Trump on his China trip, where he will meet Xi Jinping in the first US presidential visit to China in nearly 10 years.

item.title: Bosnia high representative resigns amid US backing uncertainty
item.description: Christian Schmidt, Bosnia's international peace envoy, resigned after appearing to lose US support for his role. Schmidt had held the position since 2021.

item.title: Israel strikes kill at least four in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon followed forced displacement warnings for nine towns, as the conflict between Israel and Hezbollah intensifies despite an official ceasefire.

item.title: Israeli soldiers jailed for desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
item.description: Two Israeli soldiers disciplined after one placed a cigarette in a Virgin Mary statue's mouth while the other photographed the act, drawing condemnation as an "anti-Christian" incident.

item.title: Zelensky's former chief of staff Yermak charged in major corruption probe
item.description: Andriy Yermak, who served as President Zelensky's second-in-command, faces corruption charges in a significant investigation that could reshape Ukrainian politics.

item.title: Russia paid North Korea up to $13.8 billion for war support
item.description: South Korean intelligence estimates North Korea earned $13-13.8 billion from Russia for weapons and soldiers, revealing unprecedented military cooperation between the two nations.

item.title: Ukraine and Germany sign defense cooperation agreement, plan joint drone production
item.description: Germany and Ukraine launch "Brave Germany" initiative to advance defense technologies and support innovative startups, deepening bilateral military cooperation.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strike on Baltic fuel depot prompts Latvian government resignation
item.description: A Ukrainian drone strike on an empty fuel depot in Latvia, caused by Russian GPS spoofing of Ukrainian drones, has led to a top-level resignation in the Latvian government.

item.title: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte impeached for second time
item.description: The Philippine House of Representatives impeached Vice President Sara Duterte for a second time on charges of misusing public funds and threatening the lives of President Marcos Jr and his wife. If convicted in a Senate trial, she would be disqualified from running for president.

item.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi granted bail amid health concerns
item.description: Iranian authorities granted human rights activist and Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi bail amid growing international concern over her deteriorating health in prison. Mohammadi has been a prominent voice against Iran's treatment of women.

item.title: Washington Dinner Shooting Suspect Pleads Not Guilty to Assassination Attempt on Trump
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, entered a not guilty plea after being charged with attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump during a Washington dinner event.

item.title: Elon Musk and Tim Cook to Accompany Trump on China Visit
item.description: Seventeen US executives including the Tesla and Apple CEOs will join President Trump's diplomatic visit to meet Xi Jinping, testing a fragile tariff truce between the two powers.

item.title: UK PM Starmer Pledges Stronger EU Ties in Defiant Speech
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed to put Britain at the heart of Europe in a major speech aimed at rebuilding post-Brexit relationships, as calls for his resignation grow.

item.title: Gerhard Schroeder Emerges as Putin's Pick for Ukraine Peace Mediation
item.description: The former German chancellor and close Putin ally is being positioned as a potential mediator in Ukraine peace talks, raising questions about Western acceptance.

item.title: UK Sanctions 85 Over Ukrainian Children Deportation and Armenian Election Meddling
item.description: Britain imposed one of its toughest sanctions packages targeting individuals involved in forcibly deporting and militarizing Ukrainian children, as well as those engaged in election interference in Armenia.
